title: Resettlement support for a refugee family
story:
My name is Khaled Nasser. I fled Syria with my wife and three children in 2019 after our home in Aleppo was destroyed. We have been living in Beirut's Bourj Hammoud district ever since, renting a single-room apartment for $250 per month — a sum that has become impossible to sustain since the Lebanese pound collapsed in 2020.

I worked as a mechanical engineer in Syria. In Beirut, I found informal work repairing appliances in the Mar Mikhael neighbourhood, but since the August 2020 port explosion damaged the workshop and the owner relocated to Cyprus, I have had no stable income. My wife Rana cares for our youngest, Lina (age 4), who was diagnosed with mild asthma in February 2024 at the Rafic Hariri University Hospital — an added medical cost we cannot absorb.

Our two older children, Sami (12) and Dina (10), attend UNRWA-supported schooling, but school supplies, uniforms, and transport fees totalled approximately $180 for the 2023–2024 academic year — money we borrowed from a neighbour and have not yet repaid.

As of May 2025, our landlord has issued a written notice demanding three months of overdue rent ($750) or eviction within 30 days. We have exhausted support from the local Syrian community association (Jam'iyyat Al-Tawasul Al-Souri, Bourj Hammoud branch). The association director, Mr. Elias Khoury, confirmed in writing that they have no further emergency funds available until their next quarterly disbursement in September 2025.

This campaign seeks $1,200: $750 to clear the rent arrears, $300 to cover three additional months while I secure stable employment, and $150 for Lina's next inhaler prescription and follow-up appointment. Every donor will receive a brief update once rent is settled and I have provided the landlord receipt to the campaign administrator.
vouching_narrative: The Syrian Community Solidarity Association (Jam'iyyat Al-Tawasul Al-Souri), Bourj Hammoud branch, confirms that Khaled Nasser and his family have been registered with us since October 2019 and have received three rounds of emergency food-basket assistance (December 2020, March 2022, November 2023). Our director Elias Khoury can be contacted through the association's registered office at Bourj Hammoud, Beirut. We have exhausted current emergency funds and cannot cover the rent shortfall from our quarterly disbursement cycle.
